nodes.name: Operator
nodes.summary: Human user who interacts with FlowForge through local and channel surfaces.
responsibilities: Provide prompts, credentials, confirmations, media consent, and release decisions.
interfaces: Chat Connector and other configured channels
security: Must explicitly consent to cross-channel continuity and authority-bearing changes.
observability: Receives operator-visible status, failure messages, scorecards, and release evidence.

nodes.name: CLI
nodes.summary: Local command-line surface for runtime operations, configuration, models, skills, scheduling, media, and diagnostics.
responsibilities: Provide command taxonomy, validation, and operator prompts.
Avoid platform REST loopback for product behavior.
security: Authority-bearing changes require explicit confirmation and central policy enforcement.
observability: CLI output, release gates, benchmark and soak scorecards.

nodes.name: TUI
nodes.summary: Local interactive terminal UI that should use same-process application internals rather than REST or browser-only websocket seams.
responsibilities: Render sessions, streaming, final messages, reconnect/error state, and framework-originated terminal failures.
Use shared local runtime services instead of duplicating connector behavior.
security: Must not fake daemon-live state by constructing a parallel runtime.
observability: Visible terminal failures and connection state.

nodes.name: Browser dashboard
nodes.summary: First-party browser UI that cannot import the platform internals and therefore uses dashboard websocket topics and confirmed websocket control commands.
responsibilities: Render runtime state from topic snapshots/events.
Mutate state through confirmed websocket controls.
Avoid same-origin FlowForge REST as an internal bus.
security: Authority-bearing controls require explicit operator confirmation through the central config/policy service.
observability: Connection status, toast notifications, topic refresh states, dead-letter visibility.

nodes.name: External API client
nodes.summary: Explicit integration or automation client using externally addressable FlowForge API routes.
responsibilities: Call public management, diagnostic, bootstrap, or integration endpoints intentionally.
security: Must be treated as independently permission-controlled; not trusted as an internal bus.
observability: HTTP request logs and route-level status.

nodes.name: FlowForge Platform
nodes.summary: Local-first autonomous agent runtime spanning first-party user surfaces, daemon runtime, unified pipeline, local state, media services, scheduler, and model provider integration.
responsibilities: Provide a coherent local-first agent system boundary.
Expose first-party CLI, TUI, dashboard, and channel integrations through centralized runtime behavior.
Preserve one canonical pipeline for agent turns and authority-sensitive behavior.
security: Centralized authority, connector boundaries, isolated test state, and source-backed product knowledge are system rules.

nodes.name: Integration Connectors
nodes.summary: Transport adapters for Chat Connector, Discord, email, Matrix, Signal, phone, web, A2A, and related channels.
responsibilities: Call the unified pipeline with channel context.
security: No business logic in adapters; channel allowlists and secrets must be enforced centrally or through shared services.
observability: Channel delivery logs, dead letters, channel status snapshots.

nodes.name: Daemon runtime
nodes.summary: Long-running process that wires configuration, database, LLM service, pipeline, channel router, API server, scheduler, memory, skills, MCP, plugins, and agency.
responsibilities: Wire sandbox confinement (SandboxApplier) onto the MCP manager, embedder sidecar, and browser children.
security: Service install captures explicit config path, PATH, HOME, and FLOWFORGE_* env to avoid wrong-home startup.
Supervised children (MCP stdio, embedder sidecar, browser) are confined by the Landlock applier on Linux; confinement is fail-open with a logged warning.
observability: Structured logs, startup phases, status snapshots, pipeline traces.
Boot-time embedder reachability probe result and resolved EmbeddingTier (semantic vs ngram-fallback) recorded in startup logs and surfaced on AppState for status snapshots.

nodes.name: HTTP server and API adapter
nodes.summary: Serves the dashboard, websocket endpoint, external API routes, and post-hoc observability routes.
responsibilities: Delegate domain state to shared producers/services.
security: External API routes are not internal UI buses; dashboard state uses webchannels.
observability: Request duration logs, route errors, websocket control responses.

nodes.name: Dashboard websocket topic/control plane
nodes.summary: Dashboard-private topic snapshot, event, and control command seam for browser UI state and mutations.
security: Authority-bearing commands must route through central config/policy confirmation.
observability: Control acks/errors, topic snapshots, dead-letter replay events.

nodes.name: Workflow Execution Engine
nodes.summary: Canonical factory for agent turns and the single owner of request-to-response business behavior.
responsibilities: Run decomposition, authority, memory, tools, inference, guards, post-turn ingest, and telemetry.
security: Cross-channel consent, authority policy, guards, and typed artifact proof obligations are pipeline-owned.
observability: Pipeline traces, turn diagnostics, stage liveness warnings, model/framework latency breakdown.

nodes.name: Automation Engine and Rule Router
nodes.summary: Provider abstraction, model routing, fallback, embeddings, streaming, in-flight request dedup, message sanitization, and benchmarking support.
responsibilities: Enforce provider fallback and circuit behavior.
nodes.runtime: platform service with external/local provider calls
interfaces: BuildEmbedder (local-first resolve, construct, and reachability probe)
EmbeddingClient.Rebind (in-place provider hot-swap on the one shared client, so a single reprovision activates the tier across memory, the pipeline guards, and plugin/tool routes)
security: Must sanitize malformed tool-call history at the LLM request boundary and avoid leaking secrets to providers.
observability: Provider/model fields, route traces, model latency, framework latency, fallback/circuit events.

nodes.name: External Automation Providers
nodes.summary: External LLM, embedding, and model gateway providers such as the cloud provider, OpenRouter, Moonshot, Provider A, Provider C, Provider B, Provider D, and similar configured providers.
responsibilities: Serve inference and embedding requests under provider-specific quotas, authentication, and response formats.
security: Subject to provider trust, funding, rate limits, and data retention policies.
observability: Provider errors, credit exhaustion, rate limits, route and latency attribution.

nodes.name: Embedded Automation Runtime
nodes.summary: Local model runtimes such as Embedded Runtime, llama.cpp, Docker model runner, alt-runtime-2, and related local inference surfaces.
responsibilities: Serve local model inference under host CPU/GPU/memory constraints.
Expose Embedded Runtime model-management endpoints (/api/tags, /api/pull) used for CLI model discovery, embedder provisioning, and the daemon boot-time embedding reachability probe.
interfaces: Embedded Runtime /api/tags (model list and reachability probe)
security: No cloud egress for local model calls, but host resource contention can alter outcomes.
observability: Host resource snapshots, model latency, timeout/unreachable classification.

nodes.name: Embedded SQL Store
nodes.summary: Durable local database for sessions, turns, memory, traces, config-derived runtime state, cron jobs, skills, tools, metrics, and domain repositories.
security: Tests must use isolated stores and must never mutate production state.
observability: Database health checks, quick_check, migration status, repair reports.

nodes.name: Context Store
nodes.summary: Working, episodic, semantic, relationship, retrieval, FTS/vector, curation, and post-turn ingest capabilities.
nodes.runtime: platform service backed by embedded SQL store
security: Memory retrieval must respect session scope and explicit cross-channel consent.
observability: Memory curation logs, retrieval artifacts, memory topic snapshots.

nodes.name: Scheduler and cron worker
nodes.summary: Durable cron scheduler, lease worker, heartbeat tasks, and scheduled agent execution path.
responsibilities: Execute scheduled tasks through central runtime/pipeline services.
Emit results through configured notification paths.
security: Scheduled behavior must not own agent business logic and must not silently expand agency/autonomy.
observability: Job leases, execution history, missed jobs, notification results, logs.

nodes.name: Autonomy Orchestrator
nodes.summary: Runtime-owned service for useful-work discovery, policy disposition, confirmation rules, audit records, and autonomous execution constraints.
responsibilities: Respect scopes, action classes, quiet hours, concurrency, notification policy, and token budgets.
security: Authority-bearing changes require explicit operator confirmation and audit evidence.
observability: Agency audit records, policy disposition, execution outcome, notification state.

nodes.name: Configuration and keystore
nodes.summary: Configuration loading, defaults, profiles, provider keys, encrypted keystore, and authority-bearing settings.
responsibilities: Hot-reload config file changes at runtime: poll, validate, apply across registered component reloaders with batch rollback on failure, and emit config lifecycle events.
security: Secrets must not be returned in cleartext through UI snapshots or logs.
observability: Validation output, upgrade logs, config repair output.
Config lifecycle events (file_detected / applied / validation_failed / rolled_back) logged and bridged to the dashboard event bus with a slim payload (no config snapshots).

nodes.name: Plugin Marketplace
nodes.summary: Skill inventory, skill composition, plugin registry, catalog install, activation, usage tracking, and plugin execution metadata.
responsibilities: Expose shared skill/plugin state to CLI and dashboard.
nodes.owner: internal/skillstate, internal/plugin, internal/pluginstate, internal/db skills repositories
nodes.runtime: platform service backed by filesystem and embedded SQL store
security: Plugin installs must validate manifests and avoid executing untrusted catalog assumptions.
observability: Skill usage, last-used fields, catalog errors, install results.

nodes.name: Tool Connector Hub
nodes.summary: Model Context Protocol server configuration, client management, gateway, resources, tools, and diagnostics.
responsibilities: Expose MCP tools/resources to the agent runtime.
Fetch MCP catalog metadata through runtime-owned services.
security: MCP subprocesses and remote servers require strict configuration and diagnostic boundaries.

MCP stdio children are confined on Linux via proc.Options.Sandbox (workspace = flowforge data dir, operator allow-list); confinement degrades to a logged warn-UNCONFINED on Apply failure or non-Linux platforms.
observability: MCP server test results, tool refresh state, gateway diagnostics.

nodes.name: Document and Media Services
nodes.summary: Runtime-owned STT/TTS, media device inventory/selection, camera capture, transport-media normalization, response-media egress, and canonical multimodal content handling.
responsibilities: Discover microphones, audio outputs, cameras, and capture sources.
Capture selected microphone audio for local voice drills.
Normalize Web, Chat Connector, and other transport media into canonical message parts before pipeline execution.
Normalize channel-native compressed audio such as Ogg/Opus before STT backends receive it.
Prepare optional response audio after the canonical assistant response exists.
Synthesize audio through the cross-platform Piper baseline or explicitly selected fallback backends.
Capture macOS real-camera still frames through imagesnap when ffmpeg AVFoundation is unsuitable.
Route vision-capable content through the pipeline.
nodes.runtime: platform service plus local/external media tools
security: Media capture requires permission, provenance, and retention state.
observability: Selected media devices, STT/TTS latency, camera consent state, media-processing latency, typed failures.

nodes.name: Email Integration Service
nodes.summary: inbound mail, inbound mail, outbound mail, and local bridge-compatible mail integration surface.
responsibilities: Read recent mailbox messages read-only on demand (ListRecent, BODY.PEEK, date-scoped, bounded bodies) for agent inbox summarization, distinct from inbound channel polling and never marking mail seen.
Support Proton Bridge through standard local inbound mail/outbound mail configuration.
nodes.runtime: platform service with network/local bridge endpoints
security: Mail credentials and content are critical data and require explicit configuration and redaction.
observability: Mail smoke results, typed connection/auth/send failures.
verification: Live inbound mail/inbound mail/outbound mail smoke

nodes.name: Billing and Credits Service
nodes.summary: Billing account and payment-adjacent local service surface for balances, addresses, metered-billing-related state, and service-task lifecycle evidence.
responsibilities: Own service-task status transitions and payment/failure evidence persistence.
Gate and record autonomous metered-billing micropayments (treasury policy + spend ledger), and support self-custody key import.
security: Financial actions require strong confirmation and audit boundaries.
observability: Billing account state snapshots and typed errors.

nodes.name: Observability and diagnostics
nodes.summary: Pipeline traces, request logs, model selection logs, diagnostics, flight recorder, latency breakdowns, and report-card evidence.
responsibilities: Support RCA and user-visible failure synthesis.
nodes.runtime: platform service backed by embedded SQL store/log buffers
security: Telemetry must be useful without exposing secrets or private content unnecessarily.
observability: This component is the observability owner.

nodes.name: Knowledge Base Service
nodes.summary: Source-backed retrieval service that makes the agent expert on platform setup, interfaces, integrations, troubleshooting, and release behavior without storing derivable docs as durable memory.
responsibilities: Read canonical product docs and source-derived inventories.
Build rebuildable indexes with source provenance.
Answer FlowForge product questions through the pipeline with citations.
Surface missing or stale documentation instead of hallucinating behavior.
security: Must not infer live access, credentials, or provider health without runtime evidence.
observability: Knowledge source coverage, index freshness, missing-source diagnostics.

nodes.name: Test and Release Harness
nodes.summary: Release gates, behavior soaks, model benchmarks, lint/race/test execution, manual drill evidence, and release collateral synchronization.
nodes.runtime: the platform tests, scripts, manual drills
security: Must never run tests against production state.
observability: Scorecards, logs, evidence files, release notes.

nodes.name: Engine Ingress and Session Resolver
nodes.summary: Normalizes turn input, resolves session scope, persists the user message, and preserves channel context before any model or tool behavior runs.
responsibilities: Accept canonical pipeline input from first-party and connector surfaces.
Persist the user turn before downstream execution.
security: Session scope and cross-channel consent must be resolved centrally.
observability: Turn identifiers, session identifiers, channel attribution.

nodes.name: Run Policy and Trigger Engine
nodes.summary: Classifies the current turn, derives action class and envelope policy, and carries policy-only prior context for short corrections.
responsibilities: Classify fresh external information, artifact, inspection, social, media, and correction turns.
Keep deterministic routing/classification outside the model.
security: Policy context is used for obligations, not as fabricated user text.
observability: Task synthesis events, selected profile, planned action.

nodes.name: Execution Context Builder
nodes.summary: Builds the model-facing prompt, memory context, selected tool surface, firmware, and profile-specific instructions.
security: Trust boundaries and prompt profiles must not be bypassed by connectors.
observability: Prompt length, selected tool count, tool token savings, memory presence.

nodes.name: Tool Execution and Evidence Engine
nodes.summary: Executes selected tools through shared services and records typed evidence for guards, verifiers, and user-visible claims.
responsibilities: Attach artifact, read, inspection, and media proofs.
security: Action-bearing claims require action-specific evidence or a concrete blocker.
observability: Tool call count, tool names, typed proof metadata.

nodes.name: Validation and Verification Stage
nodes.summary: Applies deterministic guard checks, verifier retries, proof obligations, and framework-originated fail-closed messages before the response is delivered.
responsibilities: Require web-read, artifact, inspection, and media evidence where applicable.
Emit clear framework errors when no model-safe answer is available.
security: Blocked framework messages must not impersonate the agent or use first person.
observability: Guard violations, verifier outcomes, retry counts, blocked reasons.

nodes.name: Post-Run Persistence and Telemetry Stage
nodes.summary: Persists final assistant output, turn diagnostics, traces, memory ingest, and dashboard notifications after finalization.
security: Post-turn work must not rewrite failed turns into unsupported success claims.
observability: Pipeline completion logs, memory ingest warnings, trace persistence.

nodes.name: Websocket topic producers
nodes.summary: Build dashboard state snapshots and incremental events from shared runtime repositories and services.
responsibilities: Publish sessions, memory, skills, agents, scheduler, observability, settings, and billing account state.
Avoid REST polling as an internal state bus.
security: Topic snapshots must redact secrets and avoid authority-bearing mutations.
observability: Topic refresh state and dead-letter replay events.

nodes.name: Websocket control dispatcher
nodes.summary: Routes typed dashboard control commands to central services and returns acknowledgements or typed errors.
security: Dangerous controls require explicit confirmation and audit through central policy.
observability: Control acknowledgements, typed errors, refresh events.

nodes.name: Autonomy Policy Engine
nodes.summary: Applies finite agency and autonomy levels, token budgets, scopes, quiet hours, action classes, confirmation rules, and safety blocks.
responsibilities: Evaluate proposed work against agency level.
Evaluate confirmation requirements against autonomy level.
security: Never-ask modes are allowed only after explicit warned operator choice; hard safety blocks still apply.
observability: Policy disposition, confirmation state, budget state.

nodes.name: Autonomy Executor
nodes.summary: Executes approved proactive work through the same central pipeline path used by user-initiated turns.
security: Scheduler-triggered agency must not execute scheduler-owned business logic.
observability: Execution outcome, notification outcome, audit records.

nodes.name: Autonomy Audit Records
nodes.summary: Durable evidence for candidate action, policy disposition, confirmation, execution result, and operator notification.
responsibilities: Support dashboard and CLI audit views.
security: Audit records must be immutable enough to explain authority-bearing actions.
observability: Candidate/action/outcome history.

nodes.name: Document Source Inventory
nodes.summary: Discovers and persists microphone, speaker, camera, and capture-device selections for voice and vision features.
nodes.runtime: the platform module plus platform media tools
security: Media use requires consent and clear provenance.
observability: Selected device, unavailable device errors.

nodes.name: Document Ingestion Service
nodes.summary: Transcribes captured or channel-native audio into canonical text before the unified pipeline handles the turn.
responsibilities: Run selected STT backend such as whisper.cpp.
Persist transcript provenance and typed failures.
nodes.runtime: the platform module plus STT executable
security: Audio processing must preserve user consent and retention decisions.
observability: STT latency, backend, transcript confidence/failure.

nodes.name: Document Rendering Service
nodes.summary: Synthesizes the finalized assistant answer through the selected voice backend after the canonical pipeline response exists.
responsibilities: Play or return audio through the active surface.
Avoid leaking markdown escape artifacts into speech.
nodes.runtime: the platform module plus Piper executable
security: TTS is output formatting and must not own agent behavior.
observability: TTS latency, backend, voice model, playback result.

nodes.name: Attachment Capture Service
nodes.summary: Captures consented still frames or video-derived frames from selected camera devices for canonical multimodal processing.
nodes.runtime: the platform module plus platform camera tool
security: Camera access requires explicit consent and retention policy.
observability: Capture provider, device, dimensions, size, latency.

nodes.name: Document Preprocessor
nodes.summary: Converts canonical image evidence into model-usable visual observations when the final route lacks direct image support.
responsibilities: Interpret image evidence with a vision-capable route/service.
Memoize repeated media interpretation within a turn.
Pass grounded visual summary into the canonical pipeline.
nodes.runtime: the platform module with LLM service dependency
security: Visual claims must be grounded in captured media evidence, not generic model capability claims.
observability: Vision model route, preprocessing latency, memoization hits.

nodes.name: Automation Route Selector
nodes.summary: Chooses an LLM route using configured preferences, provider state, capability needs, benchmark data, and runtime constraints.
security: Do not silently route sensitive data to unintended providers.
observability: Model selection events, final_model, final_provider.

nodes.name: Automation Request Sanitizer
nodes.summary: Normalizes provider-bound messages and downgrades malformed historical tool-call spans before requests leave the FlowForge boundary.
responsibilities: Strip or downgrade orphaned tool calls.
security: Malformed assistant.tool_calls history must never be sent to any model/provider.
observability: Sanitization counters, provider request diagnostics.

nodes.name: Provider Circuit and Fallback
nodes.summary: Tracks provider failures, credit/auth/rate-limit states, cooldowns, and fallback eligibility across model attempts.
responsibilities: Expose bad circuit state to diagnostics.
Distinguish retries from normal multi-attempt routing.
security: Provider health must not be reported as healthy when unconfigured, unauthorized, or credit-exhausted.
observability: Circuit state, provider error class, attempt state.

nodes.name: Benchmark and soak scorecard reporter
nodes.summary: Formats benchmark and behavior-soak results with quantitative scores, letter grades, route attribution, latency breakdowns, and qualitative notes.
responsibilities: Copy final release scorecard into release notes.
security: Report model differences and resource contention so framework conclusions are not polluted.
observability: Scores, grades, model route, latency, failures.

nodes.name: Hydrated test clone
nodes.summary: Reusable non-production clone of the operator's FlowForge environment used for realistic tests without poisoning production state.
responsibilities: Be reset from a known-good archive when dirty.
nodes.runtime: Filesystem and embedded SQL store clone
security: The clone is derived from production but must be isolated and restorable.
observability: Clone health checks, quick_check, restore evidence.

nodes.name: OS service managers
nodes.summary: launchd, systemd, Windows SCM, and process managers used to install, start, stop, and repair the FlowForge daemon.
responsibilities: Run the daemon as a service.
security: Service user, HOME, PATH, and permissions affect runtime behavior and update repair.
observability: Daemon stop/start logs, update repair output, stale sidecar reports.

nodes.name: CLI command router
nodes.summary: Maps operator CLI commands to application services without using internal REST loopback.
responsibilities: Maps operator CLI commands to application services without using internal REST loopback.

nodes.name: CLI service adapters
nodes.summary: Invoke runtime services directly from the local process boundary.
responsibilities: Invoke runtime services directly from the local process boundary.

nodes.name: TUI session runtime
nodes.summary: Runs local terminal session selection, message send, streaming, reconnect, and terminal failure presentation.
responsibilities: Runs local terminal session selection, message send, streaming, reconnect, and terminal failure presentation.

nodes.name: TUI renderer
nodes.summary: Renders terminal chat state, reconnect state, streaming messages, and user-visible framework failures.
responsibilities: Renders terminal chat state, reconnect state, streaming messages, and user-visible framework failures.

nodes.name: Daemon subsystem manager
nodes.summary: Starts, stops, and coordinates runtime subsystems owned by the daemon process.
responsibilities: Starts, stops, and coordinates runtime subsystems owned by the daemon process.

nodes.name: Daemon service control
nodes.summary: Installs, starts, stops, and validates platform service lifecycle without losing runtime context.
responsibilities: Installs, starts, stops, and validates platform service lifecycle without losing runtime context.
Emits daemon status before and after each control action as themed controlled output (not logs), so the operator sees the prior state, the resulting state, and any no-op reason.

nodes.name: Daemon background workers
nodes.summary: Runs background maintenance workers and drains them during shutdown.
responsibilities: Runs background maintenance workers and drains them during shutdown.

nodes.name: Runtime config loader
nodes.summary: Loads runtime configuration, profile state, provider order, and feature settings from the configured root.
responsibilities: Loads runtime configuration, profile state, provider order, and feature settings from the configured root.

nodes.name: Encrypted keystore adapter
nodes.summary: Stores and retrieves provider and integration secrets through the encrypted local keystore.
responsibilities: Stores and retrieves provider and integration secrets through the encrypted local keystore.

nodes.name: Dashboard SPA state model
nodes.summary: Maintains browser-local presentation state derived from websocket topic snapshots and events.
responsibilities: Maintains browser-local presentation state derived from websocket topic snapshots and events.

nodes.name: Dashboard websocket client
nodes.summary: Subscribes to runtime topics and sends approved control commands over the websocket seam.
responsibilities: Subscribes to runtime topics and sends approved control commands over the websocket seam.

nodes.name: Cron dispatcher
nodes.summary: Evaluates due cron jobs and submits work through runtime-owned execution paths.
responsibilities: Evaluates due cron jobs and submits work through runtime-owned execution paths.

nodes.name: Heartbeat runner
nodes.summary: Runs heartbeat maintenance and distributed tasks without owning agent business behavior.
responsibilities: Runs heartbeat maintenance and distributed tasks without owning agent business behavior.

nodes.name: Self-Healing Maintenance Subsystem
nodes.summary: Framework-native, deterministic maintenance/repair plane: per-domain repairers aggregated into one risk-tiered report, run on the maintenance heartbeat and on-demand.
responsibilities: Define the per-domain repairer contract (Name, Tier, Repair) and the unified SelfHealingReport.
Enforce the risk-tiered safety model centrally: low-risk DATABASE auto-applies; sensitive CONFIG (and future domains) are propose-only until operator-confirmed.
Wrap the consolidated database-repair pass (maintenance + hygiene + dead-letter summary + integrity recheck) as the auto-apply DATABASE domain.
Wrap the prompt-first config heal as the propose-only CONFIG domain.
Run with SYSTEM authority by construction (framework code; no subagent delegation, no peer-trust gate); fail loud per domain and continue the pass.
nodes.runtime: the platform framework subsystem (daemon-resident and CLI-invoked)
security: Sensitive domains (config and future workspace/plugins/skills/security) are never auto-applied; they propose and write only after explicit operator confirmation.
Runs with SYSTEM authority by construction; it is framework code and is decoupled from the peer-trust authority gate.
observability: Unified report distinguishes auto-applied vs proposed actions; each action records what was done/proposed and how to reverse it (Rule 14).
verification: internal/self-healing shell tests (tier safety, propose-vs-apply, single-domain filter, database-domain behavior preservation)

nodes.name: Public API route adapters
nodes.summary: Serve external integration API routes and avoid first-party internal loopback behavior.
responsibilities: Serve external integration API routes and avoid first-party internal loopback behavior.

nodes.name: Websocket upgrader
nodes.summary: Upgrades dashboard clients to the websocket topic/control seam.
responsibilities: Upgrades dashboard clients to the websocket topic/control seam.

nodes.name: Pipeline trace recorder
nodes.summary: Persists pipeline stages, turn diagnostics, model route details, and operator-visible RCA telemetry.
responsibilities: Persists pipeline stages, turn diagnostics, model route details, and operator-visible RCA telemetry.

nodes.name: Runtime metrics reporter
nodes.summary: Reports provider capacity, cost, latency, request logs, and benchmark/soak observability summaries.
responsibilities: Reports provider capacity, cost, latency, request logs, and benchmark/soak observability summaries.

nodes.name: Supervised process seam
nodes.summary: Single lifecycle owner for flowforge-spawned child processes (MCP stdio servers, Carbonyl browser, embedder sidecar): merged-env spawn, bounded stderr capture, context-bound kill/reap, deterministic Close, restart/health Supervisor, and the Sandbox confinement hook.
responsibilities: Spawn and reap supervised child processes with bounded stderr diagnostics.
Bind child lifetime to the caller's context and kill the process group on cancel.
Apply the configured Sandbox hook to every child before start.
Restart and health-gate the embedder sidecar via Supervisor.
security: Every supervised child is confined through proc.Options.Sandbox (security.Applier); a nil hook (sandboxing disabled) leaves the child unconfined by operator choice.
Process-group kill reaps grandchildren (e.g. chromium renderers) so confined subtrees do not orphan.
observability: Bounded stderr ring and exit status via Process.Diagnostic; Supervisor state transitions for the embedder sidecar.

nodes.name: Security sandbox
nodes.summary: OS-level process confinement: Linux Landlock filesystem enforcement applied to supervised children via a re-exec shim, with graceful warn-UNCONFINED degradation on unsupported kernels and non-Linux platforms.
responsibilities: Apply Landlock path-access restrictions on Linux through a re-exec shim that confines itself then execs the real target.
Map operator sandbox config onto the writable workspace (flowforge data dir) and allow-list.
Adapt confinement to the proc.Options.Sandbox hook and degrade loudly to UNCONFINED on failure.
nodes.runtime: In-process Linux LSM (no-op on other platforms)
security: Landlock makes the whole filesystem read+execute and grants write only to /tmp, the flowforge data dir, and the operator allow-list.
Confinement is fail-open: an Apply error or unsupported kernel logs a warning and the child runs UNCONFINED rather than being dropped.
The re-exec shim must run as the first statement of cmd.Execute so a confined child execs its target instead of re-running flowforge.
observability: Landlock ABI detection and re-exec configuration logged at debug; warn-UNCONFINED on degradation.
